danger_signals.visual_form: Players bump into each other on ice
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity to conflict; three consecutive collisions trigger fight sequence

danger_signals.visual_form: Pre-fight confrontation screen with two players facing off
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent combat; players can exchange punches; audio cue 'Fight!' announces danger state
Player losing advantage in combat; first to 5 hits receives penalty

danger_signals.visual_form: Player sent to penalty box after losing fight
danger_signals.meaning: Team disadvantage; opponent gains 5-on-4 power play opportunity

danger_signals.visual_form: Fight occurring near net triggers penalty shot
danger_signals.meaning: Reckless aggression in dangerous area results in high-risk opponent scoring chance

safety_signals.visual_form: Skaters separated on ice away from opponent
safety_signals.meaning: No immediate combat threat; normal play continues

safety_signals.visual_form: Puck transfer between teammates; audio cue 'Get the pass' or 'It's a pass'
safety_signals.meaning: Team coordination achieved; offensive momentum maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Goalie in net defending goal line
Numerical superiority on ice (5-on-4, up to 5-on-1)
safety_signals.meaning: Team in safe offensive position; increased scoring probability

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue and white (implied from jersey variation)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent rendered in contrasting jersey color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fictional team based on real NHL city; distinguishable by uniform color only

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visual differentiation through uniform color scheme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Canadian division; selectable in team choice menu

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually distinct from other factions via uniform

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single game setup screen with team selection menu
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One-off match against computer or human opponent; no tournament progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bracket-style tournament display; advancing teams shown in bracket progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple consecutive games; defeated teams eliminated; winner receives Cup award

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shootout sequence initiated; penalty shot style gameplay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Each team gets five shots; sudden-death overtime if still tied; first team to break tie wins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fight minigame screen with health bars; two players in combat stance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players exchange punches (high/low); block options available; loser penalized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Penalty shot minigame triggered; shooter on blue line facing goaltender
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One-on-one scoring attempt; unique shooting mechanics distinct from regular play

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Either Konami advertisement (bear shooting puck saying 'Nice Shot!') or Gradius minigame
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Optional minigame for Player One; promotional content breaks game flow

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reduced opposing team on ice (4 skaters vs 5); numerical disadvantage visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Penalized team plays defensively; power-play team has enhanced scoring opportunity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extreme skater disparity on ice (1 vs 5)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Penalized team severely disadvantaged; near-certain scoring opportunity for power-play team

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game reset with faceoff in liable team's zone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Possession reset; tactical shift as offending team loses positional advantage

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of eight teams rendered in distinct uniform colors to establish faction identity and opponent recognition without team logos or detailed sigils
'Blades of Steel!' on title screen establishes game identity; 'Faceoff' signals game start; 'Fight!' announces combat state; 'Penalty shot' warns of high-stakes moment

Gradius advertisement during second intermission establishes Konami universe connection; polar bear mascot provides comedic tone; regional variations (European version advertises Blades of Steel itself) show localized world context

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Defeated teams eliminated from bracket; winners advance toward Cup championship
environmental_storytelling.meaning: Visual representation of competitive hierarchy and achievement progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Eight teams from real NHL cities (Toronto, Vancouver, Montreal, Edmonton for Canada; New York, Chicago, Los Angeles, Minnesota for US) establish geographic faction identity without explicit political narrative
Game tracks goals, penalties, period progression; visual HUD reinforces competitive stakes and match progression

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Skates freely on ice; responds to controller input; moves towards puck; executes passes and shots
Three consecutive collisions without hitting others triggers confrontation state
Stops skating; faces opponent; can throw punches or choose not to; mutual punches trigger full fight sequence
Controls punch direction (high/low) and blocks (high/low); health bar depletes with each hit; losing after 5 hits triggers penalty
Sent to penalty box; removed from ice temporarily; team plays with numerical disadvantage
Positioned on blue line; controls puck approach to goaltender; unique mechanics distinct from regular shots
Stands in net; blocks incoming shots; presence reduces opponent scoring probability; can be beaten by player skill
Predictable movement; reduced aggression; easier to defeat; less effective penalty prevention
Moderate challenge; balanced aggression and defense; medium skill level
Maximum challenge; aggressive play style; effective defense and penalty execution; high scoring threat
Stops fight if occurring near net; calls penalty shot instead of fight penalty; maintains rule enforcement

notes: Blades of Steel communicates world-state primarily through: (1) jersey color as faction identifier—eight cities represented without logos, forcing players to memorize uniform colors; (2) audio cues as state transitions—'Fight!' 'Penalty shot' 'Faceoff' mark world-state changes; (3) on-ice skater count as power-play visualization—numerical advantage/disadvantage immediately visible; (4) minigame sequences as narrative breaks—intermissions pull players into Konami universe through Gradius reference and polar bear mascot. Danger is signaled through proximity mechanics (three collisions trigger fight) and fight health bars (5-hit depletion = penalty). No explicit political/faction narrative exists; teams are purely geographic representations. Regional differences (FDS vs NES vs PAL) show how advertisement and promotional content varies world-state messaging by region. The game lacks fine environmental storytelling—world-state communication relies on mechanical state changes rather than environmental decoration.
